name: Shorts
javadoc:
/** * Static utility methods pertaining to {@code short} primitives, that are not * already found in either {@link Short} or {@link Arrays}. * *

See the Guava User Guide article on * primitive utilities. * * @author Kevin Bourrillion * @since 1.0 */

members.name: saturatedCast
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns the {@code short} nearest in value to {@code value}.
 *
 * @param value any {@code long} value
 * @return the same value cast to {@code short} if it is in the range of the
 *     {@code short} type, {@link Short#MAX_VALUE} if it is too large,
 *     or {@link Short#MIN_VALUE} if it is too small
 */

tags.name: value
tags.content: any {@code long} value
tags.description: any {@code long} value

tags.content: same value cast to {@code short} if it is in the range of the {@code short} type, {@link Short#MAX_VALUE} if it is too large, or {@link Short#MIN_VALUE} if it is too small
tags.assertion: assert methodResultID == (value >= Short.MIN_VALUE && value <= Short.MAX_VALUE ? (short) value : (value > Short.MAX_VALUE ? Short.MAX_VALUE : Short.MIN_VALUE));
tags.description: the same value cast to short if it is in the range of the short type, Short#MAX_VALUE if it is too large, or Short#MIN_VALUE if it is too small

members.name: compare
members.javadoc:
/** * Compares the two specified {@code short} values. The sign of the value * returned is the same as that of {@code ((Short) a).compareTo(b)}. * *

Note for Java 7 and later: this method should be treated as * deprecated; use the equivalent {@link Short#compare} method instead. * * @param a the first {@code short} to compare * @param b the second {@code short} to compare * @return a negative value if {@code a} is less than {@code b}; a positive * value if {@code a} is greater than {@code b}; or zero if they are equal */

tags.name: a
tags.content: the first {@code short} to compare
tags.description: the first short to compare

tags.name: b
tags.content: the second {@code short} to compare
tags.description: the second short to compare

tags.content: negative value if {@code a} is less than {@code b}; a positive value if {@code a} is greater than {@code b}; or zero if they are equal
tags.description: a negative value if a is less than b; a positive value if a is greater than b; or zero if they are equal

members.name: indexOf
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns the start position of the first occurrence of the specified {@code * target} within {@code array}, or {@code -1} if there is no such occurrence. * *

More formally, returns the lowest index {@code i} such that {@code * java.util.Arrays.copyOfRange(array, i, i + target.length)} contains exactly * the same elements as {@code target}. * * @param array the array to search for the sequence {@code target} * @param target the array to search for as a sub-sequence of {@code array} */

tags.name: array
tags.content: the array to search for the sequence {@code target}
tags.description: the array to search for the sequence {@code target}

tags.name: target
tags.content: the array to search for as a sub-sequence of {@code array}
tags.description: the array to search for as a sub-sequence of {@code array}

members.name: concat
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns the values from each provided array combined into a single array.
 * For example, {@code concat(new short[] {a, b}, new short[] {}, new
 * short[] {c}} returns the array {@code {a, b, c}}.
 *
 * @param arrays zero or more {@code short} arrays
 * @return a single array containing all the values from the source arrays, in
 *     order
 */

tags.name: arrays
tags.content: zero or more {@code short} arrays
tags.description: zero or more {@code short} arrays

tags.content: single array containing all the values from the source arrays, in order
tags.description: a single array containing all the values from the source arrays, in order

members.name: toByteArray
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns a big-endian representation of {@code value} in a 2-element byte * array; equivalent to {@code * ByteBuffer.allocate(2).putShort(value).array()}. For example, the input * value {@code (short) 0x1234} would yield the byte array {@code {0x12, * 0x34}}. * *

If you need to convert and concatenate several values (possibly even of * different types), use a shared {@link java.nio.ByteBuffer} instance, or use * {@link com.google.common.io.ByteStreams#newDataOutput()} to get a growable * buffer. */

members.name: fromByteArray
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns the {@code short} value whose big-endian representation is * stored in the first 2 bytes of {@code bytes}; equivalent to {@code * ByteBuffer.wrap(bytes).getShort()}. For example, the input byte array * {@code {0x54, 0x32}} would yield the {@code short} value {@code 0x5432}. * *

Arguably, it's preferable to use {@link java.nio.ByteBuffer}; that * library exposes much more flexibility at little cost in readability. * * @throws IllegalArgumentException if {@code bytes} has fewer than 2 * elements */

tags.name: IllegalArgumentException
tags.content: if {@code bytes} has fewer than 2 elements
tags.description: if bytes has fewer than 2 elements

members.name: fromBytes
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns the {@code short} value whose byte representation is the given 2
 * bytes, in big-endian order; equivalent to {@code Shorts.fromByteArray(new
 * byte[] {b1, b2})}.
 *
 * @since 7.0
 */

members.name: ensureCapacity
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns an array containing the same values as {@code array}, but
 * guaranteed to be of a specified minimum length. If {@code array} already
 * has a length of at least {@code minLength}, it is returned directly.
 * Otherwise, a new array of size {@code minLength + padding} is returned,
 * containing the values of {@code array}, and zeroes in the remaining places.
 *
 * @param array the source array
 * @param minLength the minimum length the returned array must guarantee
 * @param padding an extra amount to "grow" the array by if growth is
 *     necessary
 * @throws IllegalArgumentException if {@code minLength} or {@code padding} is
 *     negative
 * @return an array containing the values of {@code array}, with guaranteed
 *     minimum length {@code minLength}
 */

tags.name: array
tags.content: the source array
tags.description: the source array

tags.name: minLength
tags.content: the minimum length the returned array must guarantee
tags.description: the minimum length the returned array must guarantee

tags.name: padding
tags.content: an extra amount to "grow" the array by if growth is necessary
tags.description: an extra amount to "grow" the array by if growth is necessary

tags.name: IllegalArgumentException
tags.content: if {@code minLength} or {@code padding} is negative
tags.description: if minLength or padding is negative

tags.content: array containing the values of {@code array}, with guaranteed minimum length {@code minLength}
tags.description: an array containing the values of {@code array}, with guaranteed minimum length {@code minLength}

members.name: join
members.javadoc:
/**
 * Returns a string containing the supplied {@code short} values separated
 * by {@code separator}. For example, {@code join("-", (short) 1, (short) 2,
 * (short) 3)} returns the string {@code "1-2-3"}.
 *
 * @param separator the text that should appear between consecutive values in
 *     the resulting string (but not at the start or end)
 * @param array an array of {@code short} values, possibly empty
 */

tags.name: separator
tags.content: the text that should appear between consecutive values in the resulting string (but not at the start or end)
tags.description: the text that should appear between consecutive values in the resulting string (but not at the start or end)

tags.name: array
tags.content: an array of {@code short} values, possibly empty
tags.description: an array of {@code short} values, possibly empty

members.name: lexicographicalComparator
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns a comparator that compares two {@code short} arrays * lexicographically. That is, it compares, using {@link * #compare(short, short)}), the first pair of values that follow any * common prefix, or when one array is a prefix of the other, treats the * shorter array as the lesser. For example, {@code [] < [(short) 1] < * [(short) 1, (short) 2] < [(short) 2]}. * *

The returned comparator is inconsistent with {@link * Object#equals(Object)} (since arrays support only identity equality), but * it is consistent with {@link Arrays#equals(short[], short[])}. * * @see * Lexicographical order article at Wikipedia * @since 2.0 */

tags.name: <a
tags.content: href="http://en.wikipedia.org/wiki/Lexicographical_order"> Lexicographical order article at Wikipedia

members.code:
/** * Returns a comparator that compares two {@code short} arrays * lexicographically. That is, it compares, using {@link * #compare(short, short)}), the first pair of values that follow any * common prefix, or when one array is a prefix of the other, treats the * shorter array as the lesser. For example, {@code [] < [(short) 1] < * [(short) 1, (short) 2] < [(short) 2]}. * *

The returned comparator is inconsistent with {@link * Object#equals(Object)} (since arrays support only identity equality), but * it is consistent with {@link Arrays#equals(short[], short[])}. * * @see * Lexicographical order article at Wikipedia * @since 2.0 */ public static Comparator lexicographicalComparator() { return LexicographicalComparator.INSTANCE; }

members.name: toArray
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns an array containing each value of {@code collection}, converted to * a {@code short} value in the manner of {@link Number#shortValue}. * *

Elements are copied from the argument collection as if by {@code * collection.toArray()}. Calling this method is as thread-safe as calling * that method. * * @param collection a collection of {@code Number} instances * @return an array containing the same values as {@code collection}, in the * same order, converted to primitives * @throws NullPointerException if {@code collection} or any of its elements * is null * @since 1.0 (parameter was {@code Collection} before 12.0) */

tags.name: collection
tags.content: a collection of {@code Number} instances
tags.description: a collection of {@code Number} instances

tags.content: array containing the same values as {@code collection}, in the same order, converted to primitives
tags.description: an array containing the same values as collection, in the same order, converted to primitives

tags.name: NullPointerException
tags.content: if {@code collection} or any of its elements is null
tags.description: if collection or any of its elements is null

tags.content: (parameter was {@code Collection<Short>} before 12.0)

members.name: asList
members.javadoc:
/** * Returns a fixed-size list backed by the specified array, similar to {@link * Arrays#asList(Object[])}. The list supports {@link List#set(int, Object)}, * but any attempt to set a value to {@code null} will result in a {@link * NullPointerException}. * *

The returned list maintains the values, but not the identities, of * {@code Short} objects written to or read from it. For example, whether * {@code list.get(0) == list.get(0)} is true for the returned list is * unspecified. * * @param backingArray the array to back the list * @return a list view of the array */

tags.name: backingArray
tags.content: the array to back the list
tags.description: the array to back the list

tags.content: list view of the array
tags.description: a list view of the array
